description: Weekly rotating menu offerings presented with visual emphasis and new item highlights to drive repeat visits and anticipation
structural_recipe: Post a rotating menu framework with date ranges (e.g., 'April 20-25'), highlight NEW items with emoji emphasis (🖤 MENÚ SEMANAL ROTATIVO 🖤), list specific menu items with descriptive copy, use cups/glasses/treats as visual anchors, create scarcity through limited-time positioning, include emoji separation for readability
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond to novelty and exclusivity; rotating menus create FOMO (fear of missing out) and encourage weekly check-ins. The 'NEW' positioning taps into discovery behavior. Time-limited offerings trigger urgency without aggressive sales language, aligning with cultural preferences for curated, premium experiences
transplantation_caveats: Ensure rotation dates are clearly visible and adhered to—inconsistency damages trust
New items must genuinely be new; recycled offerings reduce credibility
Verify supply chain can support rotating offerings before promoting
May not work for restaurants with limited kitchen capacity
